transcript: [laughs] Hey, uh, Hey, LPC! How’s it going? Are you—? You—you doing well?
[delay, reverb] Y—yeah! How’s everybody doin’ tonight?Glad to—
[delay, reverb] Woo! Glad to be with you. [laughs] Happy Monday, everybody. We’re gonna have a little fun tonight. Thank you so much!
Thank you, Philadelphia. Thank you very much! Please mine! [exhales]
Hi, yeah, this is Sheamus—Sheamus Blaha. How ya doin’?
I’m lookin’ for somewhere to… host a community meeting. We’re tryin’ to get a community meeting goin’ here. [exhales]
I don’t have a lotta time here, Miss. Would you be interested in hosting a community meeting if I bring all the melon and cold cuts and so on ’n so forth?
No, I’m disabled. I can’t, I’m sorry.—
Oh! [delay]Well not tonight but maybe sometime in May?
I can’t, I’m disabled. I can hardly walk.
It has to do with all the opossum and all the prairie dogs in our community, ma’am! Surely you can appreciate that, can’t ye? [delay]
Well you think about it and I’ll get back to ya. ‘Preciate your time!
Yes indeed, yes indeed! Thank you all for bein’ here tonight!—
Wanna take some questions from, ah, from the audience?
Certainly do! I certainly do! That’d be phenomenal.
So you probably wanna come down here and speak into the mic. That way he can hear you and we can be closer to the phone.
Alright, we’ve got— We’ve got some—
Hey, this is Timmy. Whatcha up to tonight? [exhales]
Timmy, Timmy, Timmy. Hey, do me a favor— Would you… bring your receptacles and your bins, uh, closer to your house? They’re kinda an eye sore out here.

You know, your bins and your receptacles and your cans, your recycling bin—all that stuff. It’s kind of an eye sore out here. Bring it in, would ya? Do the neighborhood a favor—do yourself a favor. Alright up ‘ere?—
I’m in the community and I don’t like what I see up here! Now surely you can…
…do us a favor and, uh, straighten it up a bit. You understand, am I makin’ sense, am I gettin’ thorough? [exhales] [delay]
Hey, well a good friend o’ mine’s on the City Council. Why don’t we stop by and have a few words with you on foot? [exhales]
Oh, my God! What a fuckin’ idiot you are!
Well let’s get together! [stifled laughter] Come on, rough man!

Come on, rough man. Let’s put our heads together and come up with a lil game plan here tonight. I don’t like what I see! Do. Not. Like. What I see! Am I clear, or…? Look, guy, just do us a favor and pick it up. Alright? Get out there and straighten it up.—
Pick up your bins, guy! Your garbage cans!—
It’s a mess is what it is! Alright?
It’s too close to me. It’s too close to m—
—to my property line. And I don’t like what I see! [exhales] [delay]
What’s your property line? What’s your address?
You know doggone well my address is. You’re not foolin’ anybody.
Pick it up! You got it?
What’s it gonna take for me to get through to ya? Huh? What. Is. It. Gonna take?—
I’ll get rid of it tomorrow morning.

[delay, reverb] “Tomorrow morning”? Oh, it’s— Oh, you’re full of excuses every time I turn around, eh?”Ohh… I’ll do it later. It’s not my problem.”You know, just get in gear, guy. Alright? Otherwise I’m gonna stop by and pay ya a visit on foot! ¿Comprende? or…?
Well if you come on my property then you better bring a body bag for yourself.
I’ll bring my horse whip if you try to give me any lip! [exhales] Then we’ll see who needs the bags then, tough guy, huh?[delay] Whadda ya say to this one up ‘ere?! [exhales] [delay]
Come on over then! Bring a body bag! Go on! [laughs] I’d just love to do it!
I’ll bring my bull whip and take care o’ ya—one, two, three, splat! That’s it! End o’ story!—
How’s that frickin’ sound, there, Rough Man?
Yeah, you’re gonna be layin’ on the sidewalk when I’m done with ya. I’m gonna roll ya into traffic. Whadda ya think of that one?
Well do yourself a favor—straighten it up. If you don’t I’m gonna stop by in the morning, uh, bright ’n early and pay ya a little visit. ¿Comprende?
Thank you, Philadelphia! Sorry about that! [exhales]
Uh… first of all, I’d like to say I’m an enormous fan of your work. Um…
I think one of the— One of the many things that elevate your “phone work” to an art form…

…is the sincere emotional responses that they evoke, whether it’s confusion and anger or some form of manic joy. Uh… and there’s a few calls, for me, that stand out specifically. “Super Nintendo” was a big one. One of those sort of emotional response there.Um, I was wondering if there are any—that you had—that affected you emotionally in any way.
[inhales] [sighs] Ohh! [exhales] Like in a “Stuart Smalley” sorta way? Doggone it!

Oh, think king back, ya know, it’s just a thing, uh… here and there. I’ll tell ya one thing. There was a guy who commissioned me to call his girlfriend. He’s a big listener to Longmont—loves it. And, uh… to equal proportion his girlfriend hates it—hates me. Never met me but hates me… just so much!So he actually commissioned me to call her. It’s a track called “Profiterole Frittata”. I don’t know if anyone’s at all familiar with that particular call… It’s… one of my least favorite tracks. I just fills me with… gosh, I don’t know!She’s just rude! She’s an awful… chick. I don’t know what her problem is. That one fills me with rage pretty much! [inhales]
It’s— Thank you. It’s a cornucopia of emotion is what I’m saying—to be found. But thank you so much. I’ll give it some more thought.[delay] Give it some more thought, here. Ohh-yeah! [delay]
Hey, man, I’m gonna be… uh, firin’ up my jackhammer, here. Just wanted to clear it with ya in advance.
I got my jackhammer. I’m gonna fire it up tonight and get workin’. I just wanted to… notify ya up front so there’s no surprises, alright?
Sheamus Blaha. I’m new to the area and, uh…
I got your number off a bulletin board up at the market, ya know.
Okay. What is your—? What is your address?—
So I’m gonna get started about 11 PM—my jackhammer.
Oh, I’m just catty corner to ye.
Well I wanted you to help me. I’mma have—have a party for all the neighbors. See if you wanted to help me.
Well, whenever we decide to—to do it and find out… everybody’s name.
Well I’m Sheamus Blaha, if you wanna jot that down.
Well can you email? I’ve got my hands full.
Oh, yeah? How ‘bout I come over and, uh… help myself to some snack in your kitchen!?
Thanks for calling Nicolo’s Capitol Hill. Can you hold, please?
Can I come over and, uh… fix myself some… vittles here tonight, or…?!
Uhh, okay, tonight would be better for me. It really would.
Thanks for calling Nicolo’s. How can I help you?
Hi, I need to get a… fountain drink.
Alright, you can just come get that, thanks.
Woo! [delay, pitch-shifted, speed-shifted]Woo-yeah! Aww-yeah! Aw, yeah! Oh, sure! Oh, yeah! Oh, yeah! [delay, pitch-shifted, speed-shifted]How are we tonight?
Perfect! Lay it on me! I’m… happy to be here!—

Alight. I have a question. I’ve… I’ve been wondering. You’ve always had a cult following and it’s kind of— Especially since The Age of the Internet where popularity’s growing—things like that. Especially now with the movie that we have all just seen.How good you feeling about, like… talking more about your process? Kind of like you’ve always had the aura of anonymity about you. How does it feel to talk a little bit more about what you do and share kind of, like, with the world what the process is, what you do?

Oh, thanks for asking! I’m just beginning to find out. It’s certainly uncharted… territory never having been in a movie before or having anything to do with one, any… concept really of what it involved. I mean I had a concept, sure.But, uh… ya know, lots of, uh… emails and letters coming in. Lots of, uh… well-wishers. So that part of it’s really nice. It is a bit of an adjustment but, uh… No, it’s been really positive so far. It’s been a rarity up to this point so that’s another positive, ya know?
Hey, I’m—I’m doing well. How are you doin’?
Not too bad! What’s been happenin’?
Not much! I’m watchin’ a movie about Bronies right now. Um…
And, uh… Yeah. You know who’s been on my mind lately, is Alex Trebek and I’d wond— I was wondering if you’d gotten ahold of him since the whole diagnosis.
Oh yeah, I’ve talked to him a couple times.
Did he seem to be in good spirits?
Definitely. Definitely. He’s no worse for the wear at least, ya know, verbally sounding, but yeah! Was happy to talk to him.—
He was happy to pick up. So no, I felt weird callin’ him—
—but he’s— He’s still dishin’ it out, ya know?
Yeah? Di—? Is he—? Did he have any, like, shipments on—?
—on the way to his house or anything when you called him?
[laughs] Is that gonna be on the next record?
He was, uh, preparing a shipment, actually.
What’re you doin’, guy? I’m supposed to meet you outside. What’re you doin’? What’re you waitin’ for?
I’ve been outs— I’ve been outside.
Yeh? Ring my fuckin’ bell, then. Come over already!
Hey man, can you cut—cut it with the language? I’ve got my kid on the line, here.
You “got your kid on the line”? [laughs] Ring my fuckin’ bell!—
I’m gonna wr— I’m gonna wring your neck, guy, is what I’m gonna do!
Wring my neck, then, ya little dickhead!
Yeah! Fuck you, scumbag! Ring my bell.—
What’re you prepared to do about it, there, guy?
Ring my bell and you’ll see! [laughs]
You’re gonna be seein’ stars, guy, once I’m done pummelin’ ya! Ya feel me?
